message:
Ever notice how your dog can't just flop down and sleep? 🐶 First comes the ritual: the slow lap around the bed, two or three turns, a thoughtful pause… and FINALLY they collapse into the exact spot they were standing on the whole time. 😅 So what's with the little merry-go-round?

It's an ancient instinct, passed down from their wild ancestors long before cozy dog beds existed. Out in the open, a wolf couldn't just lie down anywhere. Circling let them tamp down tall grass, brush, or snow into a comfortable nest — and that trampling did double duty, chasing off any snakes, bugs, or critters hiding in the spot before they settled in.

That spin was also a quick safety check. Turning in a full circle gave them one last scan of their surroundings, a chance to note where the rest of the pack was, and a moment to point their nose into the wind to sniff for any danger drifting their way. And in cold weather, winding into a tight, curled ball helped them tuck in and trap precious body heat through the night.

Your pup has a memory-foam bed and a climate-controlled living room — zero snakes, zero predators — and yet that hardwired little ritual still runs every single night. 🥰 Thousands of years of the wild, performed nightly on a fluffy cushion.

How many spins does YOUR dog do before they finally settle? Count them tonight and tell me below! 🐾
